was the 110th season of professional [rugby league](/wiki/Rugby_league "Rugby league") in Australia and the 20th season run by the [National Rugby League](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League"). The season started in [New Zealand](/wiki/New_Zealand "New Zealand") with the annual [Auckland Nines](/wiki/2017_NRL_Auckland_Nines "2017 NRL Auckland Nines"), and was followed by the [All Stars Match](/wiki/2017_All_Stars_match "2017 All Stars match"), which was played at [McDonald Jones Stadium](/wiki/Newcastle_International_Sports_Centre "Newcastle International Sports Centre") in Newcastle,[[1]](#cite_note-1) and the [World Club Series](/wiki/2017_World_Club_Series "2017 World Club Series"). It marked the last time that the [Anzac Test](/wiki/2017_Anzac_Test "2017 Anzac Test") and [City vs. Country](/wiki/City_vs_Country_Origin "City vs Country Origin") representative matches were played.

The **2017 NRL season** was the 110th season of professional [rugby league](/wiki/Rugby_league "Rugby league") in Australia and the 20th season run by the [National Rugby League](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League"). The season started in [New Zealand](/wiki/New_Zealand "New Zealand") with the annual [Auckland Nines](/wiki/2017_NRL_Auckland_Nines "2017 NRL Auckland Nines"), and was followed by the [All Stars Match](/wiki/2017_All_Stars_match "2017 All Stars match"), which was played at [McDonald Jones Stadium](/wiki/Newcastle_International_Sports_Centre "Newcastle International Sports Centre") in Newcastle,[[1]](#cite_note-1) and the [World Club Series](/wiki/2017_World_Club_Series "2017 World Club Series"). It marked the last time that the [Anzac Test](/wiki/2017_Anzac_Test "2017 Anzac Test") and [City vs. Country](/wiki/City_vs_Country_Origin "City vs Country Origin") representative matches were played.

## Pre-season

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2017_NRL_season&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Pre-season")]

The 2017 pre-season featured the fourth edition of the [Auckland Nines](/wiki/2017_NRL_Auckland_Nines "2017 NRL Auckland Nines") competition, held over a weekend at Auckland's [Eden Park](/wiki/Eden_Park "Eden Park") in which the [Sydney Roosters](/wiki/Sydney_Roosters "Sydney Roosters") defeated the [Penrith Panthers](/wiki/Penrith_Panthers "Penrith Panthers") in the final. The [All Stars match](/wiki/2017_All_Stars_match "2017 All Stars match") was held on February 10 at [McDonald Jones Stadium](/wiki/Newcastle_International_Sports_Centre "Newcastle International Sports Centre") in Newcastle. The [2017 World Club Series](/wiki/2017_World_Club_Series "2017 World Club Series") took place in England with the [Super League champions](/wiki/Super_League_XXI "Super League XXI") [Wigan Warriors](/wiki/Wigan_Warriors "Wigan Warriors") defeating the [NRL premiers](/wiki/2016_NRL_season "2016 NRL season") [Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks](/wiki/Cronulla-Sutherland_Sharks "Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks") in the [World Club Challenge](/wiki/World_Club_Challenge "World Club Challenge") match.

## Player statistics and records

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2017_NRL_season&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Player statistics and records")]

* [Cameron Smith](/wiki/Cameron_Smith_(rugby_league,_born_1983) "Cameron Smith (rugby league, born 1983)") became the most prolific goal kicker in NRL history when he kicked his 943rd goal in Round 9. During the Finals he surpassed [Darren Lockyer](/wiki/Darren_Lockyer "Darren Lockyer") as the most capped player in NRL history with his 356th game and became the first player to kick 1,000 NRL goals.
* [Nathan Cleary](/wiki/Nathan_Cleary "Nathan Cleary"), 19, broke the record for the youngest player to score 200 points in a season which was set by [Graham Eadie](/wiki/Graham_Eadie "Graham Eadie"), 20, in 1974. Cleary also became the youngest player to be the season's leading points scorer since [Harold Horder](/wiki/Harold_Horder "Harold Horder") in 1913.
* [Kirisome Auva'a](/wiki/Kirisome_Auva%27a "Kirisome Auva'a") scored the fastest try in NRL history in 13 seconds for [Parramatta Eels](/wiki/Parramatta_Eels "Parramatta Eels") against [Brisbane Broncos](/wiki/Brisbane_Broncos "Brisbane Broncos") in Round 25.
